name: Persian Gulf Patrol
objective: Complete a hot hypersaline patrol that tests whether the hull, welds, and seals can survive accelerated corrosion, pitting, and corrosion-fatigue before breach.

environmental_profile.notes: Extreme hypersaline hot-water patrol. High conductivity, low pH, and thermal cycling accelerate pitting corrosion, seal degradation, and corrosion-fatigue crack growth.

rationale: The Persian Gulf is calm compared with storm missions, but this patrol is chemically brutal. Salinity of 48 ppt combined with 35°C average water, 41°C hot-water peaks, and depressed pH creates an aggressive electrochemical corrosion environment. The kill chain is slow but severe: high salinity -> accelerated corrosion -> pitting -> corrosion-fatigue crack initiation -> hull breach -> sink.
